plan_units.title: Module 1 Completion and Forward Bridge
plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize Module 1 completion and articulate the transition from innovator mindset work into Module 2 leadership-identity work using the bridge language in the source narration.
plan_units.rationale: This is the core function of the bridge slide and directly establishes the handoff to Module 2.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From Module 1 closure to Module 2 readiness through a concise leadership-focused bridge.
source_refs: Module 1 Complete: The Mindset is Set.
In Module 2, we will move from Mindset to Identity.

plan_units.title: What Module 1 Has Equipped the Learner to See
plan_units.learning_objective: Summarize the Module 1 conceptual carry-forwards that the learner should bring into the Module 2 handoff, especially system change, opportunity identification, and diagnostic innovation thinking.
plan_units.rationale: The bridge explicitly references prior learning and the supporting source pillar document clarifies which Module 1 ideas are valid carry-forwards.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From recalled Module 1 concepts to a disciplined handoff inventory through selective synthesis of the module's core pillars.
source_refs: You have mapped out the macro forces changing medicine,
you have filled your Opportunity Radar
You are no longer just reacting to the system;

plan_units.title: Opportunity Identification Depends on Root-Cause Discipline
plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that Opportunity Radar targets are only meaningful when grounded in systemic diagnosis, root-cause analysis, and problem-first thinking from Module 1.
plan_units.rationale: This deepens the Module 1 recap with the most operational concepts that support an assessment-driven bridge without introducing new topic matter.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From recalled Module 1 concepts to a disciplined handoff inventory through selective synthesis of the module's core pillars.
source_refs: a leader should not buy or build a solution
Falling in Love with the Problem

plan_units.title: Psychological Safety as a Condition for Honest System Diagnosis
plan_units.learning_objective: Connect Module 1's emphasis on psychological safety to the learner's readiness to continue into leadership identity work.
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-value conceptual bridge because it links diagnosis of broken systems with the interpersonal conditions leadership must later sustain.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From recalled Module 1 concepts to a disciplined handoff inventory through selective synthesis of the module's core pillars.
source_refs: too afraid to report what is actually broken
Without a shared belief that it is safe

plan_units.title: The Central Tension: A Good Idea Is Not Enough
plan_units.learning_objective: Explain the bridge claim that opportunity viability depends not only on the idea but on the leader who can champion it inside a complex health system.
plan_units.rationale: This is the central transition claim in the narration and deserves clustering because it carries multiple explanatory beats and high pedagogical weight.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in ideas to recognition of leadership dependence through an explicit contrast between opportunity and champion.
source_refs: But an idea is only as good as the leader who champions it.
What are the core characteristics you need to cultivate
an opportunity is only as viable as the leader championing it

plan_units.title: Why the Other 90% Is Leadership Execution
plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the leadership capabilities named in the bridge as essential for moving ideas through a matrixed health system.
plan_units.rationale: The narration lists the exact capability categories that define the Module 2 handoff, and those phrases should be preserved.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in ideas to recognition of leadership dependence through an explicit contrast between opportunity and champion.
source_refs: Having the right idea is only 10% of the battle;
the other 90% is having the political savvy,
to get others to follow you

plan_units.title: From System Reaction to System Redesign
plan_units.learning_objective: Reframe the learner's role from reacting within the system to preparing to redesign it, using the bridge's motivational identity shift.
plan_units.rationale: This is the motivational resolution of the central tension and directly supports the forward-looking transition into Module 2.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in ideas to recognition of leadership dependence through an explicit contrast between opportunity and champion.
source_refs: You are no longer just reacting to the system;
you are preparing to redesign it.
to get others to follow you

plan_units.title: Module 2 Preview: Identity, Communication, and Inspiration
plan_units.learning_objective: Preview the specific Module 2 focus areas named in the bridge narration: defining oneself as a leader, communicating vision, and inspiring others to join.
plan_units.rationale: These are the explicit incoming competencies for Module 2 and therefore necessary for assessment-aligned handoff planning.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From abstract leadership need to concrete Module 2 expectations through explicit preview of the next module's focus areas.
source_refs: You will begin to define who you are as a leader,
how you can inspire others to join your cause.

plan_units.title: The Final Call Forward
plan_units.learning_objective: Close the bridge by framing Module 2 as preparation to build the leader who will solve the waiting clinical problem.
plan_units.rationale: This is the bridge's closing takeaway and should land as the final resolve beat for the lesson plan.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From abstract leadership need to concrete Module 2 expectations through explicit preview of the next module's focus areas.
source_refs: it is time to build the leader who will solve it.

plan_units.title: Assessment Bridge Reflection Checkpoint
plan_units.learning_objective: Use the module knowledge-check concepts to frame a brief reflection on which Module 1 assessment ideas the learner should carry into Module 2 leadership-identity work.
plan_units.rationale: The operator framing requests an assessment-driven bridge, and the assessment corpus provides valid source-grounded recall targets for this checkpoint.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assessed recall of Module 1 to intentional Module 2 preparation through a compact reflection checkpoint.

plan_units.title: Reflection Prompt: Which Concepts Shape Your Leadership Readiness?
plan_units.learning_objective: Prompt learners to identify which Module 1 concepts most shape their readiness for leadership identity formation in Module 2.
plan_units.rationale: This uses only source-grounded concepts and serves as the first reflective elaboration of the assessment bridge.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assessed recall of Module 1 to intentional Module 2 preparation through a compact reflection checkpoint.
source_refs: What data did this failure give us

plan_units.title: Reflection Prompt: From Problem Diagnosis to Leadership Identity
plan_units.learning_objective: Help learners connect diagnostic tools and problem-first innovation habits from Module 1 to the kind of leader they must become in Module 2.
plan_units.rationale: This is the core transfer move between modules and remains fully grounded in the source corpus.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assessed recall of Module 1 to intentional Module 2 preparation through a compact reflection checkpoint.
source_refs: the true underlying root cause, or Lead Domino
Falling in Love with the Problem

plan_units.title: Reflection Prompt: Readiness for the Module 2 Handoff
plan_units.learning_objective: Conclude the assessment bridge by having learners name the leadership capabilities they now need to cultivate next.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat closes the reflection sequence and cleanly hands off to Module 2's identity work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assessed recall of Module 1 to intentional Module 2 preparation through a compact reflection checkpoint.
source_refs: inspire others to join your cause

lesson_summary: A concise assessment-bridge lesson that closes Module 1, selectively recaps the module's most transferable innovation concepts, introduces the key tension that ideas require leadership to succeed, previews Module 2's identity/communication/inspiration focus, and ends with assessment-grounded reflection prompts that support the handoff into leadership-identity work.

sections.title: Module 1 Carry-Forwards to Bring Into Module 2

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded prose summary of the major Module 1 pillars referenced in the bridge, including intrapreneurship inside hospitals, systemic diagnosis, root cause thinking, Opportunity Radar, and psychological safety.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: Short synthesis of what the learner has already mapped and identified.

exercises.prompt_intent: Summarize which Module 1 concepts feel most essential to carry forward into leadership-identity work.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the richer explanatory bridge that would overcrowd slides, helping learners revisit the module's key concepts before the leadership turn.

sections.title: Why Leadership Determines Whether Opportunity Becomes Change

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanation of the contrast between having an idea and successfully championing it in a complex matrixed health system, including the named capabilities from the narration.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The core bridge claim that a good idea is not enough.

exercises.prompt_intent: Analyze which named leadership capability may be most limiting for a clinician-innovator trying to move an idea through a hospital system.
exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Having the right idea is only 10% of the battle;

sections.narrative_intent: Deepen the central bridge argument without overloading the close/preview slide sequence.

sections.title: Previewing the Work of Module 2

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller learner-facing preview of defining leadership identity, communicating vision, and inspiring others, framed as upcoming work rather than already-taught content.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The short list of Module 2 focus areas.

exercises.prompt_intent: Draft a personal handoff note naming one opportunity from Module 1 and one leadership competency to develop in Module 2.

sections.narrative_intent: Convert the Module 2 preview into a more deliberate transition artifact learners can use between modules.

sections.title: Assessment Reflection Bridge

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Workbook reflection space tying selected knowledge-check concepts to learner self-assessment and module handoff decisions.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: Compact reflection prompts and handoff cues.

exercises.prompt_intent: Explain how one assessed Module 1 concept changes what kind of leader the learner now needs to become.
Apply one assessed Module 1 framework to a future leadership challenge anticipated in Module 2.

sections.narrative_intent: Make the assessment bridge tangible by turning recall into intentional readiness planning.

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner may benefit from a future primary-source grounding pack for the macro-force claims referenced in the bridge, but this bridge plan does not add new topic content.
Learner may benefit from future examples of leadership behaviors inside matrixed health systems to enrich Module 2, while keeping this bridge source-locked to the current corpus.
